title: FEMS hygiene manager job: consolidate + prune + flag + promote (folded WP-1-FEMS-Hygiene-Manager-Job)
goal: Implement a scheduled FEMS hygiene job that runs consolidation, pruning, flagging, and procedural promotion via a cost-effective model (no LLM in the runtime path; LLM only as opt-in step for consolidation summarization). Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Hygiene-Manager-Job. Ports the battle-tested Memory Manager pattern from repo governance into the product Execution / Job Runtime.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/hygiene.rs (HygieneJobRunner + HygieneTask + HygieneReport + ProceduralPromotion types). Wires into the existing Execution / Job Runtime (consumed by trait so no new scheduler). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/hygiene_manager_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-157 (bitemporal indexing - hygiene needs to invalidate items, not delete), MT-158 (outcome feedback - hygiene uses outcome history for promotion decisions), MT-159 (pinned core - hygiene must not prune pinned items).
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Hygiene-Manager-Job preserved_intent and refinement D.3 'hygiene manager job for procedural promotion / GC'. Surface: `pub struct HygieneJobRunner<'a> { fems: &'a dyn FemsAccessor, action_catalog: &'a dyn KernelActionSubmitter }` with `pub fn run_once(config: HygieneConfig) -> Result<HygieneReport, HygieneError>`. HygieneTask enum: `Consolidate { max_pairs: u32 }`, `PruneStale { older_than: Duration, min_score: f64 }`, `FlagContradictions`, `PromoteProcedural { min_use_count: u32, min_pass_rate: f64 }`. HygieneReport: per-task `{ items_visited, items_acted_on, errors, duration_ms }`. Consolidation: find near-duplicate pairs (using existing FEMS embedding distance) and emit MemoryConsolidationCandidate via KernelActionCatalogV1 for review-gated merge; DO NOT auto-merge. Prune: items older than threshold AND score below min_score get invalidated (set invalidated_at via MT-157 bitemporal axis, not deleted). MUST skip pinned items (MT-159). Flag contradictions: pairs of items with high cosine similarity but contradicting outcomes (one PASS-attributed, one FAIL-attributed) flagged for operator review. Promote procedural: items used >=min_use_count times with PASS rate >=min_pass_rate become candidates for ProceduralPromotion (operator review then surface promotion). NO LLM required in the runtime path; an optional consolidation summarizer is opt-in only and runs out-of-band. Tests: each HygieneTask runs independently; pruned items get invalidated_at set (NOT deleted); pinned items skipped; consolidation emits candidates without auto-merging; report aggregates per-task counts.

POSTGRES_TEST_URL='postgres://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:5432/postgres?sslmode=disable' CARGO_TARGET_DIR='D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target' cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test hygiene_manager_tests -- --ignored --test-threads=1
risk_if_missed: Without active hygiene FEMS LongTermMemory grows unbounded, stale items pollute MemoryPack injection, and contradictions accumulate.
validator_focus: Hygiene never auto-merges or auto-deletes; all mutations are review-gated via KernelActionCatalogV1. Pruning uses bitemporal invalidation, not destructive delete. Pinned items always skipped.

proof.note: Focused hygiene manager check passed with POSTGRES_TEST_URL.

implementation_evidence.summary: MT-160 implemented a deterministic FEMS hygiene manager with independent consolidation, prune-candidate, contradiction-flag, and procedural-promotion tasks. Runtime hygiene never calls an LLM, never auto-merges, and never auto-deletes; all candidate mutations are emitted through KernelActionCatalogV1/MemoryBox review gates.

sources_checked: LangGraph memory overview: semantic, episodic, procedural memory and background memory updates
Zep/Graphiti temporal graph memory documentation and maintenance source
Mem0 memory add API and async queued processing documentation
Deep Agents memory and human-in-the-loop documentation
CoALA, Generative Agents, and MemP procedural-memory papers
patterns_found: Background memory maintenance should stay separate from hot inference paths.
Memory hygiene should version, invalidate, or emit review candidates rather than destructively deleting state.
Contradictions should be flagged with provenance and review context.
Procedural memory promotion is high risk and should be gated by evidence and review.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse MT-157 bitemporal FEMS visibility/invalidation surface.
Reuse MT-158 outcome counters and pass-rate signals.
Reuse KernelActionCatalogV1 and PostgresKernelActionSubmitter review-gated MemoryBox mutation path.
rejected_options: Rejected runtime LLM summarization in hygiene jobs because the MT requires no LLM in the runtime path.
Rejected direct auto-prune invalidation from run_once because validator focus requires all hygiene mutations to be review-gated.
Rejected SQLite/offline fixture paths because the WP is PostgreSQL-only.
research_basis.selected_approach: Deterministic background hygiene runner backed by FemsAccessor and HygieneActionSubmitter traits; production wiring uses PostgresBitemporalMemoryIndex and PostgresKernelActionSubmitter. Hygiene emits review-gated KernelActionCatalogV1 candidates and reports counts.
risks: Existing bitemporal payloads without outcome, score, pinned, or embedding fields may produce conservative defaults and under-emit candidates.
Full crate warnings remain outside the MT-160 changed surface.
Broad unscoped cargo test commands can build unrelated integration targets and time out on this repository.
mitigations: Tests cover pinned skip behavior, invalid-threshold rejection before FEMS reads, review-gated prune candidate emission, embedding-similarity consolidation, catalog registration, and live Postgres candidate persistence.
Workflow unit test was rerun with --lib scoping and passed.
Static diff scans verified no added SQLite lines and no hygiene runtime LLM calls; the only added LLM marker is runtime metadata declaring llm_runtime_required=false.
validation_plan: Run scoped rustfmt check on touched Rust files.
Run ignored live Postgres hygiene submitter test with POSTGRES_TEST_URL.
Run handshake_core library check with test-utils.
Run the workflow-routing unit test under --lib.

behavior_notes: Consolidation uses FEMS embedding cosine similarity when both items have embeddings, with fingerprint fallback for legacy or test items.
PruneStale emits a review-gated prune candidate and does not call bitemporal invalidation inside run_once; approved invalidation remains available through FemsAccessor::invalidate.
FlagContradictions skips pinned items and flags high-similarity PASS/FAIL outcome conflicts.
PromoteProcedural skips pinned items and emits ProceduralPromotion candidates when use_count and pass_rate thresholds are met.
The memory_hygiene_v0.1 job protocol is routable without memory_items inputs and returns a hygiene_report plus review state.

out_of_owned_files_touched_pending_opera.why: Extended D.3 integration coverage for hygiene behavior and propagated the new HygieneItemView embedding field.
Registered review-gated hygiene catalog actions required by the MT validator focus.
Implemented the production Postgres hygiene action submitter and payload projection.
Registered the new hygiene_manager_tests integration target under the existing test-utils feature.
Routed memory_hygiene_v0.1 through the existing job runtime without adding a scheduler.
Allowed the memory_hygiene_v0.1 protocol to parse as a FEMS job protocol.

minimum_controls: Hygiene never auto-merges or auto-deletes; all mutations are operator-review-gated via KernelActionCatalogV1.
Pruning uses bitemporal invalidation (sets invalidated_at) instead of destructive delete so replay still works.
Pinned items always skipped; hygiene cannot collide with operator pin decisions.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: HygieneJobRunner never auto-merges or auto-deletes; every task emits review-gated candidates through KernelActionCatalogV1. Pruning correctly emits a Prune candidate rather than calling invalidate. Each task explicitly skips pinned items. However, pinned state is sourced from the bitemporal item payload, and pin/unpin actions live in a separate aggregate; without payload projection, production hygiene cannot see IPC-pinned items and could emit prune/consolidate/flag/promote candidates against them.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: HygieneJobRunner never auto-merges or auto-deletes; every task emits review-gated candidates through KernelActionCatalogV1. Pruning correctly emits a Prune candidate rather than calling invalidate. Each task explicitly skips pinned items. However, pinned state is sourced from the bitemporal item payload, and pin/unpin actions live in a separate aggregate; without payload projection, production hygiene cannot see IPC-pinned items and could emit prune/consolidate/flag/promote candidates against them.
